mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate signals an abrupt demand contraction that triggers corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off portfolio rebalancing, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power beyond a 1-week horizon in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Constrained industrial output combined with elevated consumer price inflation compresses corporate profit margins through input cost pass-through failures, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both proxies are in the top quartile of their historical distribution, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses the 0.5% threshold during an active inflation fight, markets price in aggressive liquidity withdrawal, which suppresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral after the rate threshold is breached, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent GDP growth surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated personal savings balances indicate resilient household balance sheets that sustain consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when RSAFS is in the top quartile, or the signal flips to negative predictive power during periods of high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below the 100 index baseline signals a broad manufacturing recession that reduces corporate capex and earnings guidance, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat when INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal shows no incremental explanatory power over a rolling 12-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in monthly payroll employment and housing starts reflects synchronized labor market and construction demand, which amplifies corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a 5-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth drives upward revisions to corporate earnings estimates, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher during GDP acceleration phases, or the signal produces negative returns in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized.
